quotes.title: Clear the Ground
quotes.challenge: Food, eat one food without craving, pressure, or guilt and enjoy it.
quotes.prompt: What becomes available when you stop pretending that this feeling is not there?
quotes.quote: The wound is the place where the Light enters you.
quotes.author: Rumi

supplemental.title: Day 1 Companion Video
supplemental.description: A short companion video example for the first day of release work.

quotes.title: Let Go a Little
quotes.challenge: Choose one happy memory, and feel deep, lingering gratitude for it for for a whole minute.
quotes.prompt: Where did your body soften when you let go even a little?
quotes.quote: If you let go a little, you will have a little peace. If you let go a lot, you will have a lot of peace.
quotes.author: Ajahn Chah

supplemental.title: Day 2 Companion Podcast
supplemental.description: A podcast example for listening while practicing release and peace.

quotes.title: Feel Without Fixing
quotes.challenge: Listen to one of your favorite up-beat songs and dance to it simply for the joy of it
quotes.prompt: What did the feeling seem to need from you: witness, truth, rest, action, or compassion?
quotes.quote: Nothing ever goes away until it has taught us what we need to know.
quotes.author: Pema Chodron

supplemental.title: Day 3 Companion Article
supplemental.description: A written companion example for reflecting before trying to fix a feeling.

quotes.title: Choose a Clean Slate
quotes.challenge: Choose one part of your body that you appreciate, and share gratitude for it, sending positive messages and feelings to it aloud or in your mind for a minute
quotes.prompt: What would today look like if the past could inform you without directing you?
quotes.quote: The partial becomes complete; the crooked, straight; the empty, full; the worn out, new.
quotes.author: Laozi

supplemental.title: Day 4 Companion Resource
supplemental.description: A resource hub example for choosing a clean slate and continuing the work.

quotes.title: Find the Belief
quotes.challenge: Send a quick message of thanks to someone who has loved you without judgement, and hold appreciation for them while you write it.
quotes.prompt: Which belief feels inherited, outdated, or too small for who you are becoming?
quotes.quote: What is necessary to change a person is to change his awareness of himself.
quotes.author: Abraham Maslow

supplemental.title: Day 5 Companion Audio
supplemental.description: An audio example for noticing inherited beliefs and making space for a new one.

quotes.title: Ask a Better Question
quotes.challenge: Identify one feeling you've been resisting, and lean into it for a minute, and pay attention to what it needs from you: rest, forgiveness, compassion, surrender, acceptance, or awareness.
quotes.prompt: How did the quality of your question change the quality of your energy?
quotes.quote: All that we are is the result of what we have thought: it is founded on our thoughts, it is made up of our thoughts.
quotes.author: Dhammapada

supplemental.title: Day 6 Companion Video
supplemental.description: A general video example for practicing better questions and truthful next steps.

quotes.title: Rewrite the Story
quotes.challenge: Take 5, deep "belly breaths" when you begin to feel stuck, trapped, or anxious, calling your power back into your present.
quotes.prompt: What words felt both empowering and true enough to practice?
quotes.quote: You never change things by fighting the existing reality. To change something, build a new model that makes the existing model obsolete.
quotes.author: Buckminster Fuller

quotes.title: Practice the New Pattern
quotes.challenge: Think of a time when you followed your intuition and the outcome was remarkable. Then breathe in deep gratitude for your intuition.
quotes.prompt: What action made the new belief feel more real?
quotes.quote: Moral excellence comes about as a result of habit. We become just by doing just acts, temperate by doing temperate acts, brave by doing brave acts.
quotes.author: Aristotle

quotes.title: Protect Your Peace
quotes.challenge: What is something your intuition has encouraged you to take action for, but you haven't followed through?
quotes.prompt: What helped you stay present without absorbing everything around you?
quotes.quote: Keep thy heart with all diligence; for out of it [are] the issues of life.
quotes.author: Solomon

quotes.title: Strengthen Your Boundary
quotes.challenge: Spend only 2 minutes taking action in something you've been intuitively inspired to do. (Making plans for it doesn't count). Even if the action is imperfect, take 2 minutes taking action. Set the timer and keep it to 2 minutes.
quotes.prompt: What did the boundary make possible for your energy or focus?
quotes.quote: No person is your friend who demands your silence, or denies your right to grow.
quotes.author: Alice Walker

quotes.title: Small Routine Upgrade
quotes.challenge:
What's one limiting belief about your worth or value that you want to let go of to open more abundance into your life? Feel the emotion, and then surrender it to your Higher Power. 
Pro tip: When it begins to surface again, acknowledge it and then surrender it again, until it's no longer part of your mentality.
quotes.prompt: What routine gave your day more structure without making it rigid?
quotes.quote: Mindfulness allows you to live deeply every moment that is given you to live.
quotes.author: Thich Nhat Hanh

quotes.title: Stand in Your Space
quotes.challenge: What is one feeling that comes up when you consider following your intuition as your primary guide for your life and business?
quotes.prompt: How did you recognize the difference between pressure and real strength?
quotes.quote: For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.
quotes.author: Paul

quotes.title: Listen for Guidance
quotes.challenge: In what way do you feel blocked in accomplishing your current ambition or goal? Write it down and mentally hand it (surrender it) to God or your Higher Power to handle it, and exercise faith and trust to truly surrender that obstacle while still moving forward in your goal.
quotes.prompt: What did you hear before your mind tried to edit it?
quotes.quote: Go to your bosom: Knock there, and ask your heart what it doth know.
quotes.author: William Shakespeare
quotes.source: Measure for Measure, Act II, Scene II

quotes.title: Test the Next Step
quotes.challenge: 14) For two whole minutes, allow yourself to feel the emotion "joy" without any particular reason (you can start with gratitude, and then think of moments you have felt joy and allow your whole body to feel joy).
quotes.prompt: What did your body seem to know before you had a full explanation?
quotes.quote: He who knows other men is discerning; he who knows himself is intelligent.
quotes.author: Laozi

quotes.title: Trust Your Inner Knowing
quotes.challenge: 15) Accept this truth into your heart, even if it feels foreign or not quite true. Allow your heart to feel it for 90 seconds without rejecting it: "I am created for success, and each step of my life, even setback, is propelling me toward greater success."
quotes.prompt: What makes an intuitive nudge feel trustworthy to you?
quotes.quote: The power of intuitive understanding will protect you from harm until the end of your days.
quotes.author: Lao Tzu

quotes.title: Make Room for Receiving
quotes.challenge: 16) What is one life priority that has been neglected? Is it worth focusing on right now, or is it worth surrendering right now to free your time for something else? Write down your impressions, and choose to let go all guilt, shame, feelings of failure or overwhelm related to it.
quotes.prompt: What changes when receiving becomes an active practice instead of a passive hope?
quotes.quote: Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you:
quotes.author: Matthew

quotes.title: Notice the Block
quotes.challenge: 17) Think of one time-consuming distraction or habit in your life that is inhibiting personal development. What is one "joyful" or soul-nourishing experience that you can choose to do FIRST, before you engage in the distraction or habit? (Bringing more joy into your life and surrendering the guilt, shame, failure, and overwhelm about those activities will have a greater impact that using only willpower to change a habit.)
quotes.prompt: What might that block be trying to protect you from?
quotes.quote: The real enemies of our life are the oughts and the ifs.
quotes.author: Henri Nouwen

quotes.title: Ask for Support
quotes.challenge: 18) Think of one food or drink that you crave. Write it down. Consider that there may exist a more joyful food or drink that will better nourish your body and bring you better health and vitality, and take a minute to write down your thoughts and impressions.
quotes.prompt: What belief about support surfaced when you asked?
quotes.quote: Rarely, if ever, are any of us healed in isolation. Healing is an act of communion.
quotes.author: bell hooks

quotes.title: Receive the Good
quotes.challenge: When something good arrives today, pause before minimizing it and let it register.
quotes.prompt: What does receiving goodness bring up in you?
quotes.quote: All shall be well, and all shall be well, and all manner of thing shall be well.
quotes.author: Julian of Norwich

quotes.title: Practice Generous Trust
quotes.challenge: Choose one generous thought about your future, your resources, or your ability to be supported.
quotes.prompt: Where did scarcity loosen when you practiced trust?
quotes.quote: When one door closes, another opens; but we often look so long upon the closed door that we do not see the one which has opened.
quotes.author: Alexander Graham Bell

quotes.title: Track Your Energy
quotes.challenge: Notice three things today: what energizes you, what depletes you, and what brings you back to center.
quotes.prompt: What did your energy reveal about your priorities?
quotes.quote: By the way we live our daily life we contribute to peace or to war.
quotes.author: Thich Nhat Hanh

quotes.title: Align with What Matters
quotes.challenge: Choose one action that reflects your actual priority, not the loudest demand.
quotes.prompt: What matters most right now, and what action proves it?
quotes.quote: Your right is only to work, but not to its results; do not let the results of action be your motive, nor let your attachment be to inaction.
quotes.author: Bhagavad Gita

quotes.title: Choose the High-Return Step
quotes.challenge: Pick the one next step that gives the best return for your life, energy, or purpose today.
quotes.prompt: What did you choose after separating urgency from importance?
quotes.quote: If it is not right, do not do it: if it is not true, do not say it.
quotes.author: Marcus Aurelius

quotes.title: Make Space for the Plan
quotes.challenge: Remove, delay, or delegate one low-alignment task so there is room for what matters.
quotes.prompt: What did you learn from making space instead of adding more?
quotes.quote: My method is different. I do not rush into actual work.
quotes.author: Nikola Tesla

quotes.title: Name Your Gifts
quotes.challenge: List three gifts, abilities, or forms of wisdom that feel natural enough you sometimes overlook them.
quotes.prompt: Which gift is ready to be taken more seriously?
quotes.quote: As every man hath received the gift, [even so] minister the same one to another, as good stewards of the manifold grace of God.
quotes.author: Peter

quotes.title: Claim Your Resources
quotes.challenge: Identify one resource you already have: a relationship, skill, experience, insight, tool, or opportunity.
quotes.prompt: What resource becomes more powerful when you stop dismissing it?
quotes.quote: When you do the common things in life in an uncommon way, you will command the attention of the world.
quotes.author: George Washington Carver

quotes.title: Honor Your Importance
quotes.challenge: Act today as if your presence, gifts, and choices genuinely matter.
quotes.prompt: What changes when you stop treating your contribution as optional?
quotes.quote: Act as if what you do makes a difference. It does.
quotes.author: William James

quotes.title: Use What You Have
quotes.challenge: Use one existing gift or resource today instead of waiting for a perfect condition.
quotes.prompt: What grew stronger because you used it?
quotes.quote: For unto every one that hath shall be given, and he shall have abundance: but from him that hath not shall be taken away even that which he hath.
quotes.author: Matthew

quotes.title: Explore Your Essence
quotes.challenge: Write five words that describe who you are beneath roles, pressure, and performance.
quotes.prompt: Which word felt most alive or surprising?
quotes.quote: There is no greater agony than bearing an untold story inside you.
quotes.author: Maya Angelou
quotes.source: I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings

quotes.title: Invite Both Minds
quotes.challenge: Let your practical mind and spiritual knowing both respond to one decision before you choose.
quotes.prompt: What did logic notice, and what did inner wisdom notice?
quotes.quote: This above all: to thine own self be true.
quotes.author: William Shakespeare

quotes.title: Follow the Aliveness
quotes.challenge: Notice what feels alive, meaningful, or quietly magnetic, then take one respectful step toward it.
quotes.prompt: What does aliveness teach you about your path?
quotes.quote: Don't ask what the world needs. Ask what makes you come alive, and go do it.
quotes.author: Howard Thurman
quotes.source: The Living Wisdom of Howard Thurman

quotes.title: Bless Your Uniqueness
quotes.challenge: Celebrate one trait, sensitivity, story, or way of seeing that makes your path your own.
quotes.prompt: What if this difference is not a detour but part of the design?
quotes.quote: I will praise thee; for I am fearfully [and] wonderfully made: marvellous [are] thy works; and [that] my soul knoweth right well.
quotes.author: David

quotes.title: Connect Essence and Service
quotes.challenge: Name one way your natural gifts could meet a real need in another person's life.
quotes.prompt: Where do enjoyment, ability, and usefulness overlap for you?
quotes.quote: Do your little bit of good where you are; it's those little bits of good put together that overwhelm the world.
quotes.author: Desmond Tutu

quotes.title: Let Value Flow
quotes.challenge: Reflect on one way money can represent value exchanged, needs met, and gifts put into motion.
quotes.prompt: What changes when money is part of flow rather than proof of worth?
quotes.quote: Try not to become a man of success, but rather try to become a man of value.
quotes.author: Albert Einstein

quotes.title: Serve from Strength
quotes.challenge: Offer something today from overflow, clarity, or real willingness instead of proving yourself.
quotes.prompt: How does service feel different when it comes from strength rather than obligation?
quotes.quote: And God [is] able to make all grace abound toward you; that ye, always having all sufficiency in all [things], may abound to every good work:
quotes.author: Paul

quotes.title: Receive for a Purpose
quotes.challenge: Write one sentence about what more support, money, time, or energy would allow you to create or contribute.
quotes.prompt: What purpose makes receiving feel cleaner and more grounded?
quotes.quote: Love begins at home, and it is not how much we do, but how much love we put in the action that we do.
quotes.author: Mother Teresa

quotes.title: Review Your Tools
quotes.challenge: List the three tools from this journey that helped you most: release, questions, intuition, boundaries, priorities, support, or service.
quotes.prompt: Which tool deserves a permanent place in your rhythm?
quotes.quote: Rise, awake! having obtained your boons, understand them!
quotes.author: Katha Upanishad

quotes.title: Check In for Momentum
quotes.challenge: Choose one recurring check-in that will keep your growth alive after day forty.
quotes.prompt: How often do you need to pause, listen, clear, and realign?
quotes.quote: For indeed, with hardship [will be] ease.
quotes.author: Qur'an

quotes.title: Honor How Far You Have Come
quotes.challenge: Name three ways you are not relating to yourself, abundance, or purpose the way you did before.
quotes.prompt: What change deserves to be witnessed before you rush into the next thing?
quotes.quote: I walk slowly, but I never walk backward.
quotes.author: Abraham Lincoln

quotes.title: Choose What Comes Next
quotes.challenge: Write one grounded next step and one blessing over the person you are continuing to become.
quotes.prompt: What do you now know about abundance, alignment, and your own free will?
quotes.quote: Go confidently in the direction of your dreams. Live the life you have imagined.
quotes.author: Henry David Thoreau
